name: Tranco de Beas Reservoir Loop from Villanueva del Arzobispo
name_original: Villanueva Del Arzobispo – vuelta por Tranco y Beas de Segura
description: This difficult 51.8-mile road cycling route offers significant climbs and stunning mountain scenery in Sierra de Cazorla Natural Park.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villanueva Del Arzobispo are situated within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features significant elevation changes with challenging mountain ascents and quiet roads that traverse pine forests, alpine plateaus, and extensive olive groves. This landscape provides a variety of road cycling experiences, from demanding climbs to routes along the Guadalquivir River. Best road cycling routes around Villanueva Del Arzobispo The most popular road cycling route is Tranco de Beas Reservoir Loop from Villanueva del Arzobispo, a 51.8 miles (83.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the scenic reservoir area within the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Embalse del Tranco de Beas and ascent to Mirador de Huelguecillas — circular from Hornos, a difficult 46.8 miles (75.3 km) path. This route features an ascent to a viewpoint and circumnavigates the Tranco de Beas reservoir, offering mountain scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Ascent to El Yelmo — circular route around the Tranco Reservoir, a 40.3 miles (64.9 km) trail leading through the mountainous terrain around the Tranco Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 58 minutes. Road cycling around Villanueva Del Arzobispo is defined by the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, vast olive groves, and quiet mountain roads. The network offers options for both challenging ascents and more moderate paths. The routes in Villanueva Del Arzobispo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva Del Arzobispo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villanueva Del Arzobispo?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Villanueva Del Arzobispo, offering a wide range of experiences for cyclists. These include 20 moderate routes and 34 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in this region?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from challenging mountain ascents within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park to quieter roads traversing extensive olive groves and pine forests. While most routes are paved, some segments might be unpaved, offering a mixed experience. You'll find significant elevation changes, ideal for those seeking demanding climbs.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Villanueva Del Arzobispo?
A: Yes, many routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009289917/tranco-de-beas-reservoir-loop-from-villanueva-del-arzobispo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir Loop from Villanueva del Arzobispo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult route that explores the scenic reservoir area. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1345010776/ascent-to-el-yelmo-circular-route-around-the-tranco-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to El Yelmo — circular route around the Tranco Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging ride through mountainous terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes in the area often pass by stunning natural and historical sites. You can visit the natural spot of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Pringue&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Cabeza de la Viña Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabueno Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also traverse the vast Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, offering breathtaking mountain scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling around Villanueva Del Arzobispo?
A: The region is generally favorable for cycling, but the best time depends on your preference. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures, ideal for longer rides and challenging climbs. Summers can be hot, especially in the valleys, but higher elevation routes within the Sierra de Cazorla Natural Park might provide cooler conditions. Winters are generally mild, but some higher mountain passes could be colder.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation changes and are classified as moderate to difficult, the region offers some easier touring paths, such as those along the Guadalquivir River within the natural park. These flatter sections can be more suitable for families or those seeking a less strenuous ride. Always check the route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Villanueva Del Arzobispo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the quiet mountain roads, the stunning landscapes of pine forests and olive groves, and the diverse challenges offered by the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park. The opportunity to tackle classic climbs like 'Puerto de Tíscar' and 'El Yelmo' is also a significant draw.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Villanueva Del Arzobispo?
A: Villanueva Del Arzobispo and the surrounding villages within the natural park typically offer various parking options. Look for designated parking areas in town centers or near popular trailheads. For routes starting from specific points, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Aceite – Tranco de Beas Reservoir loop from Antigua Estacion de tren&lt;/a&gt;, parking might be available near the starting point.

Q: Are there any routes that feature natural water spots or lakes?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate natural water features. The Tranco de Beas Reservoir is a prominent feature in many cycling tours, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1345001169/embalse-del-tranco-de-beas-and-ascent-to-mirador-de-huelguecillas-circular-from-hornos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Tranco de Beas and ascent to Mirador de Huelguecillas — circular from Hornos&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find the natural spot of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Pringue&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured in routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009362084/charco-de-la-pringue-return-via-the-ascent-of-los-masegosos-and-cortijos-nuevos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco De La Pringue – return via the ascent of los Masegosos and Cortijos Nuevos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to rest along the road cycling routes?
A: Many cycling routes pass through or near charming rural villages, which often have local cafes, restaurants, or small shops where you can refuel and rest. Villanueva Del Arzobispo itself has amenities, and smaller settlements within the Sierra de Cazorla Natural Park also offer opportunities for breaks. It's always a good idea to plan your route and check for services in advance.

Q: What are some of the most challenging climbs for road cyclists in the region?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers several notable climbs. The Sierra de Cazorla features significant elevation changes, with classic ascents such as 'Puerto de Tíscar,' 'Las Palomas,' and 'El Yelmo.' Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1345010776/ascent-to-el-yelmo-circular-route-around-the-tranco-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to El Yelmo — circular route around the Tranco Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; provide a taste of these demanding mountain roads.
tips: The Tranco de Beas reservoir collects the waters of the Guadalquivir river in the Sierra de Segura. The path follows the shoreline of the reservoir, giving you spectacular views of the surroundings.
Small town located on the A-6301 road that crosses the town between Sierra Morena and Sierra de Segura. The area is the largest protected natural space in Spain.
The town of Villanueva del Arzobispo is located in the Sierra de las Cuatro Villas, surrounded by olive groves and cereal fields.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Villanueva Del Arzobispo – return via Tranco and Beas de Segura road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult, with a 'medium-high level' of challenge. It features significant climbs and mountainous terrain, requiring good to very good fitness. The total elevation gain is approximately 1,365 meters over 83 kilometers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: You will primarily ride on mostly paved surfaces along quiet mountain roads. The terrain is mountainous, with numerous peaks and significant ascents. While the main route is suitable for racebikes, some parts of the broader region may include unpaved sections better suited for mountain bikes, but this specific route sticks to paved roads.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular mountain scenery and passes through several notable locations. You'll enjoy views of the Tranco de Beas Reservoir, ride through the settlement of Beas de Segura, and start/finish in the Villanueva del Arzobispo Town Center. Expect picturesque whitewashed Andalusian villages, vast olive groves, and dramatic limestone crags.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, allowing you to avoid the intense heat of the summer months and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Villanueva Del Arzobispo – return via Tranco and Beas de Segura route?
faqs.answer: Villanueva del Arzobispo serves as an excellent starting and ending point for this route. You should be able to find public parking options within the Villanueva del Arzobispo Town Center or nearby areas, suitable for leaving your vehicle while you cycle.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or special regulations for cycling in the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. However, as Europe's largest protected natural area, it's important to always respect park regulations, stay on designated routes, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve its natural beauty.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant elevation gain, and 83-kilometer distance, this specific route is not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires a good level of fitness and experience with road cycling in mountainous terrain. Shorter, less challenging routes may be available elsewhere in the park for those seeking an easier ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. You will find portions that are part of the Transandalus, the CIMA JA10 Iznatoraf * Puente Ortega, and the Presa del Tranco - Cañada Morales. These intersections can offer opportunities to explore alternative or extended rides if desired.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through settlements like Villanueva del Arzobispo Town Center and Beas de Segura, you will find options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. These towns are known for their Andalusian culture and local cuisine, providing convenient stops for refreshments or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: Is the Villanueva Del Arzobispo – return via Tranco and Beas de Segura route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so while dogs are generally allowed in public areas of the towns you pass through, bringing a dog along for the entire 83-kilometer racebike route is typically not practical or safe. If you plan to visit the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park with a dog, it's best to check specific park regulations regarding pets on trails or in protected areas, as rules can vary.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I see while cycling?
faqs.answer: The route is set within Europe's largest protected natural area, the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. While cycling, you'll be immersed in dense pine forests and pass through areas with vast olive groves. Keep an eye out for various bird species and other local wildlife that inhabit this rich natural environment, though direct encounters can be less common on main roads.
